news.headline: US-Iran War Escalation: US Strikes Kharg Island — Crude Surges 7% This Week
news.what_happened: The US military attacked Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export terminal, on July 7. Trump declared the ceasefire 'over.' EU aviation authority (EASA) advised airlines to avoid Iran, Iraq, and Lebanon airspace until August 31.
news.why_it_matters: WTI crude has surged from ~$68 to $74.30 in days. India imports 85% of crude oil — this directly widens India's current account deficit and raises fuel, plastic, and petrochemical input costs across multiple sectors. Airlines face dual pressure: higher jet fuel costs AND route disruptions from Middle East airspace closures.

news.headline: TCS Q1 FY27 Results Today Post Market — Revenue Flat, Margin Under Pressure
news.what_happened: TCS will announce Q1 FY27 results post 3:30 PM IST. Revenue expected at ~Rs 72,012 Cr (muted 1.9% growth), EBIT margin may contract 150 bps Q-o-Q due to wage hikes. 9 large deals won including SKF mega deal; AI revenue at $1.3B annualized. Interim dividend likely.
news.why_it_matters: TCS is the bellwether of Indian IT. Results set the tone for INFY, WIPRO, HCLTECH and the sector. A margin miss or strong deal wins could swing TCS 3-5% in tomorrow's session. Today TCS stock may be volatile in anticipation.

news.headline: FII Net Buyers Rs 1,962.80 Cr + DII Rs 790.20 Cr on July 8 — Dual Institutional Support
news.what_happened: Foreign institutional investors were net buyers of Rs 1,962.80 Cr in cash segment on July 8. Domestic institutions added another Rs 790.20 Cr. Both sides buying simultaneously.
news.why_it_matters: Strong dual institutional support suggests underlying demand despite the GIFT Nifty gap-down signal. Combined Rs 2,753 Cr institutional buying provides a floor to large-cap Nifty stocks even in a negative-open scenario.

news.headline: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged at 5.25% — Neutral Stance Maintained
news.what_happened: RBI MPC unanimously decided to retain the repo rate at 5.25% with a neutral stance. FY27 GDP growth projected at 6.9%, CPI inflation at 4.6%. SDF at 5%, MSF at 5.50%. Cybersecurity advisory issued for regulated entities.
news.why_it_matters: No rate cut this cycle — rate-sensitive sectors (Real Estate, NBFCs, Auto) get no monetary tailwind. Banks retain current NIM levels. Market stays in wait-and-watch mode on monetary policy.

sector_outlook.reason: WTI crude +7% this week on US-Iran war; ONGC and OIL are direct realization beneficiaries
Inventory gains from rising crude for BPCL, HPCL, IOC; short-term positive
Crude surge raises jet fuel costs; EASA airspace ban over Iran/Iraq/Lebanon adds route disruption
Crude-derived raw materials (TiO2, solvents) costs rising with oil surge
Synthetic rubber and carbon black (crude derivatives) input costs rising sharply
Nasdaq +0.20%, USD/INR flat; TCS results binary event post market today
RBI on hold at 5.25%; US 10Y at 4.57% modest headwind; FII buying supportive
Crude rise increases plastic packaging and petrochemical input costs
Nikkei +2.42% positive but China Shanghai fell 0.49%; mixed demand signals
Defensive but no fresh catalyst; USD/INR flat limits export earning uplift

picks.catalyst: US-Iran war: US struck Kharg Island (Iran's main oil terminal); WTI crude at $74.30 — up 7% this week
picks.logic: ONGC is India's largest upstream oil producer. Every $1 rise in crude realization improves EBITDA by ~Rs 1,500-2,000 Cr. With WTI surging from ~$68 to $74+ on geopolitical escalation, ONGC Q2 FY27 realization improves significantly. Direct P&L beneficiary — no indirect link. Prev close Rs 244.18.

picks.catalyst: US-Iran war escalation; WTI crude at $74.30 — Oil India directly benefits from higher crude realization
picks.logic: Oil India Ltd is India's second largest upstream oil & gas producer with ~21% domestic crude production share. Net realization is directly linked to international crude prices. With WTI at multi-week highs on Middle East supply fears, OIL Q2 FY27 earnings see meaningful uplift. Higher beta than ONGC to oil price moves. Prev close Rs 423.15.

risks: GIFT Nifty -1.24%: Nifty may open 440+ pts lower — wait for gap-down to stabilize before entering watch zones
US-Iran conflict is unpredictable: any ceasefire announcement reverses crude move instantly, impacting ONGC/OIL negatively
TCS results post market today — if margins disappoint broadly, IT sector and Nifty sentiment could worsen overnight
USD/INR at 95.55 — crude-driven CAD widening may pressure rupee further, adding to cost-push inflation
US 10Y at 4.57%: Fed staying higher-for-longer restricts FII inflows to EMs; broad market upside cap
eod_summary: Both picks missed targets: ONGC -2.38% (target +4%), OIL -2.16% (target +4%). Despite bullish crude thesis, market gap-down overwhelmed oil stock gains. Nifty closed at 23962.80 (-1.79% vs prev close).
